vocab.example: I still lean towards paper books because the tactile sensation of turning a page is far more satisfying than swiping a screen.

vocab.meaning: sự mỏi mắt do sử dụng thiết bị điện tử
vocab.example: Reading on a screen for prolonged periods inevitably leads to digital eye strain and headaches.
E-readers offer unparalleled convenience, allowing me to carry an entire library in my backpack.
Electronic devices lack the haptic feedback that makes physical books feel more substantial and personal.

vocab.meaning: sự phát xạ ánh sáng xanh
vocab.example: I avoid reading on tablets before bed due to the blue light emission, which can disrupt my sleep cycle.

vocab.meaning: yếu tố về tính di động
vocab.example: The portability factor of digital devices is a game-changer for commuters who want to read on a crowded train.
For me, reading a physical book is a holistic sensory experience, involving the scent of the paper and the weight of the volume.

vocab.meaning: khả năng điều chỉnh vô hạn (font, độ sáng...)
vocab.example: The infinite adjustability of e-books, such as changing font sizes, is a huge boon for people with visual impairments.

vocab.meaning: khả năng tìm kiếm thông tin
vocab.example: When studying, I prefer digital formats for their searchability, as I can find specific keywords in seconds.
Switching to digital reading has kept my living room clutter-free, as I no longer need massive bookshelves for every novel.
